section.contentHints: Full-screen watercolor landscape video, floating title logo, 'Watch Trailer' play button.

designPlan.role: Page opener and value proposition anchor
designPlan.goal: Transport the user into the film's world within seconds.
designPlan.roleFit: Immediately satisfies the visitor's goal to 'Experience the film's atmosphere'.
designPlan.taskLoopFocus: The 'View immersive hero animation' and 'Watch trailer' steps.
designPlan.capabilityFocus: Demonstrates the 'Immersive Visual Experience' capability through high-fidelity video.
designPlan.informationArchitecture: Centered title and primary action over a full-bleed background.
designPlan.layoutIntent: Centered editorial layout with minimal UI clutter.
designPlan.visualIntent: Cinematic, using a 'living painting' video background with soft ambient motion.
designPlan.interactionIntent: Large, inviting play button that opens a modal trailer.
designPlan.contentStrategy: Lead with the film's title and a poetic tagline.
constraints: Video must be muted and loop seamlessly
Title must be legible over moving video
designPlan.rationale: A centered hero with ambient video is the most effective way to establish the Ghibli-esque 'sense of place'.

pageContext.title: Home
pageContext.description: The main promotional gateway for the film.

pageDesignPlan.pageGoal: The main promotional gateway for the film.
pageDesignPlan.roleFit: This page primarily serves Potential Viewer.
pageDesignPlan.capabilityFocus: This page should make Immersive Visual Experience, Ticketing & Lead Gen understandable and actionable.
pageDesignPlan.taskLoopCoverage: Help users progress through Discovery to Conversion, Discovery to Conversion, Discovery to Conversion, Discovery to Conversion.
pageDesignPlan.narrativeArc: Move from orientation to persuasion, then social proof, and finish with a decisive conversion close.
pageDesignPlan.layoutStrategy: Use section rhythm, spacing, and contrast to support the entry stage of the user journey rather than making every block equally loud.
hierarchy: The first viewport should establish context and value quickly.
Mid-page sections should deepen trust or explain the offer.
Lower-page sections should reduce friction and reinforce action.
pageDesignPlan.transitionStrategy: Make transitions feel intentional through spacing, contrast shifts, and restrained motion rather than decorative separators.
sharedShellNotes: Assume global layout sections frame the page experience.
Keep page-specific sections visually coherent with the shared shell.
constraints: Preserve the given section order unless a wrapper or grouping is required for coherence.
Avoid repetitive section pacing; vary density and emphasis across the page.
pageDesignPlan.rationale: Default page planner framed Home around role needs and the MVP journey rather than isolated sections.
